schemes.name: PM-KISAN
schemes.description: Pradhan Mantri Kisan Samman Nidhi. A central sector scheme providing income support to all landholding farmers' families to supplement their financial needs for procuring various inputs related to agriculture and allied activities.
schemes.eligibility: All landholding farmers' families with cultivable land. Exclusions apply for institutional landholders, former/current constitutional post holders, government employees, and professionals like doctors, engineers, lawyers paying income tax.
schemes.benefits: Rs. 6,000 per year in three equal installments of Rs. 2,000 each, directly transferred to bank accounts.

schemes.name: Ayushman Bharat - PMJAY
schemes.description: Pradhan Mantri Jan Arogya Yojana is the world's largest health insurance scheme fully financed by the government. It provides health coverage for secondary and tertiary care hospitalization to poor and vulnerable families.
schemes.eligibility: Families identified based on deprivation and occupational criteria as per SECC 2011 data. Includes families with no adult member between 16-59 years, female-headed households, disabled members, SC/ST households, landless households, manual scavengers, etc.
schemes.benefits: Health insurance cover of Rs. 5 lakhs per family per year for secondary and tertiary care hospitalization across public and empaneled private hospitals in India. Cashless and paperless access to services.

schemes.name: PM Awas Yojana - Gramin (PMAY-G)
schemes.description: Housing for All (Rural) scheme aims to provide pucca houses with basic amenities to all houseless and those living in kutcha and dilapidated houses in rural areas by 2024.
schemes.eligibility: Houseless families or those living in houses with kutcha walls and kutcha roof, with 0, 1, or 2 room houses as per SECC 2011 data. Priority to SC/ST, freed bonded laborers, minorities, widows, and physically handicapped.
schemes.benefits: Financial assistance of Rs. 1.20 lakh in plain areas and Rs. 1.30 lakh in hilly/difficult areas. Additional Rs. 12,000 for toilet construction under SBM. 90 days of unskilled labor wages under MGNREGA.

schemes.name: PM Awas Yojana - Urban (PMAY-U)
schemes.description: Housing for All (Urban) mission to provide affordable housing to urban poor including slum dwellers through credit linked subsidy, affordable housing in partnership, and beneficiary-led construction.
schemes.eligibility: EWS (annual income up to Rs. 3 lakh), LIG (Rs. 3-6 lakh), MIG-I (Rs. 6-12 lakh), MIG-II (Rs. 12-18 lakh). Beneficiary family should not own a pucca house anywhere in India.
schemes.benefits: Interest subsidy: EWS/LIG - 6.5% on loan up to Rs. 6 lakh; MIG-I - 4% on loan up to Rs. 9 lakh; MIG-II - 3% on loan up to Rs. 12 lakh. Direct assistance of Rs. 1.5 lakh for EWS in slum redevelopment.
documents: Affidavit of not owning pucca house

schemes.name: Mudra Loan (PMMY)
schemes.description: Pradhan Mantri Mudra Yojana provides loans up to Rs. 10 lakh to non-corporate, non-farm small/micro enterprises. Three categories: Shishu (up to Rs. 50,000), Kishore (Rs. 50,000 to Rs. 5 lakh), and Tarun (Rs. 5 lakh to Rs. 10 lakh).
schemes.eligibility: Any Indian citizen with a business plan for non-farm income generating activity such as manufacturing, processing, trading, or service sector. Includes shopkeepers, vendors, artisans, and small manufacturers.
schemes.benefits: Collateral-free loans: Shishu - up to Rs. 50,000; Kishore - Rs. 50,000 to Rs. 5 lakh; Tarun - Rs. 5 lakh to Rs. 10 lakh. No processing fee for Shishu loans. Repayment period up to 5 years.

schemes.name: PM Kaushal Vikas Yojana 4.0 (PMKVY)
schemes.description: Flagship skill development scheme to enable Indian youth to take up industry-relevant skill training that helps them secure better livelihood. Focuses on fresh skilling, upskilling, and reskilling.
schemes.eligibility: Indian nationals, especially unemployed youth, school/college dropouts, 10th/12th pass students. Age typically 15-45 years depending on course. No educational qualification for certain courses.
schemes.benefits: Free skill training with certification. Training allowance of Rs. 500 per month. Placement assistance. Recognition of Prior Learning (RPL) for existing skills. Industry-recognized certification.
schemes.target_group: Unemployed youth, school dropouts, workers seeking skill upgradation

schemes.name: Ujjwala Yojana 2.0 (PMUY)
schemes.description: Pradhan Mantri Ujjwala Yojana aims to safeguard health of women and children by providing clean cooking fuel (LPG) to poor households. Version 2.0 covers migrant families and requires minimal documentation.
schemes.eligibility: Adult women from poor households not having LPG connection. Priority to SC/ST, PMAY beneficiaries, AAY, forest dwellers, tea garden workers, and residents of islands. For 2.0: migrants can apply with self-declaration.
schemes.benefits: Free LPG connection with first refill and stove. Financial support of Rs. 1,600 per connection. Subsidy on subsequent refills. Option for EMI payment for stove and first refill.
schemes.target_group: BPL women, poor households, migrant families

schemes.name: Startup India
schemes.description: Flagship initiative to build a strong ecosystem for nurturing innovation and startups in India. Provides various benefits including tax exemptions, easier compliance, IPR support, and funding access.
schemes.eligibility: Entity incorporated as Private Limited Company, Registered Partnership, or LLP. Should be less than 10 years old from date of incorporation. Annual turnover should not exceed Rs. 100 crore. Working towards innovation/improvement of products/services.
schemes.benefits: 3-year income tax exemption (Section 80-IAC). Self-certification compliance for 6 labor and 3 environment laws. 80% rebate on patent filing. Fast-track patent examination. Fund of Funds access. Easy winding up process.

schemes.name: Stand-Up India
schemes.description: Facilitates bank loans between Rs. 10 lakh and Rs. 1 crore to at least one SC/ST borrower and one woman borrower per bank branch for setting up greenfield enterprises in manufacturing, services, or trading sectors.
schemes.eligibility: SC/ST and/or women entrepreneurs above 18 years. Should not be in default to any bank/financial institution. For non-individual enterprises, 51% controlling stake should be held by SC/ST or woman entrepreneur.
schemes.benefits: Composite loan of Rs. 10 lakh to Rs. 1 crore including term loan and working capital. Repayment period up to 7 years with moratorium of up to 18 months. Margin money of up to 25% from convergence with other schemes.

schemes.name: Beti Bachao Beti Padhao
schemes.description: National campaign to address declining child sex ratio and related issues of women empowerment through multi-sectoral intervention. Focuses on prevention of gender-biased sex selection, ensuring survival and protection of girl child, and education.
schemes.eligibility: All families with girl children. Scheme implemented through convergence of various departments. Benefits through various sub-schemes and programs targeting girls and women.
schemes.benefits: Awareness campaigns, community mobilization, improved enforcement of PC-PNDT Act, multi-sectoral action in selected districts. Access to education, nutrition, and health services for girls. Financial incentives through convergent schemes.

schemes.name: Sukanya Samriddhi Yojana (SSY)
schemes.description: Small savings scheme for girl child under Beti Bachao Beti Padhao campaign. Offers highest interest rate among all post office savings schemes with tax benefits. Helps parents build corpus for daughter's education and marriage.
schemes.eligibility: Account can be opened for girl child below 10 years of age by natural or legal guardian. Only one account per girl child. Maximum two accounts in a family (exception for twins/triplets).
schemes.benefits: Current interest rate: 8.2% p.a. (subject to quarterly revision). Minimum deposit: Rs. 250/year, Maximum: Rs. 1.5 lakh/year. Tax benefits under Section 80C. Maturity on girl attaining 21 years. Partial withdrawal (50%) allowed after age 18 for education.
documents: 2 Passport size photographs of girl and guardian
Medical certificate for multiple birth cases

schemes.name: PM Jeevan Jyoti Bima Yojana (PMJJBY)
schemes.description: One-year life insurance scheme renewable annually, offering coverage for death due to any reason. Available through banks and post offices for people having savings bank accounts.
schemes.eligibility: Indian citizens between 18-50 years of age with a savings bank account. Auto-debit consent required. Coverage continues till age 55 if enrolled before 50.
schemes.benefits: Life cover of Rs. 2 lakhs in case of death due to any reason. Premium of Rs. 436 per annum (auto-debited). Coverage period: 1st June to 31st May. Claim settlement to nominee.
documents: Savings Bank Account linked with Aadhaar

schemes.name: PM Suraksha Bima Yojana (PMSBY)
schemes.description: One-year accident insurance scheme renewable annually, offering coverage for death or disability due to accident. Most affordable accident insurance scheme with premium of just Rs. 20 per year.
schemes.eligibility: Indian citizens between 18-70 years of age with a savings bank account. Auto-debit consent required for annual premium.
schemes.benefits: Rs. 2 lakhs for accidental death or total permanent disability. Rs. 1 lakh for partial permanent disability. Premium: Rs. 20 per annum (auto-debited). Coverage: 1st June to 31st May.

schemes.name: Atal Pension Yojana (APY)
schemes.description: Government-backed pension scheme for unorganized sector workers. Provides guaranteed minimum pension of Rs. 1,000 to Rs. 5,000 per month after 60 years of age depending on contribution.
schemes.eligibility: Indian citizens between 18-40 years of age. Must have savings bank account and linked mobile number. Should not be income tax payer. Not a member of any statutory social security scheme.
schemes.benefits: Guaranteed monthly pension of Rs. 1,000/2,000/3,000/4,000/5,000 after age 60. Same pension to spouse after subscriber's death. Return of accumulated corpus to nominee after both deaths. Government co-contribution for eligible subscribers.

schemes.name: National Pension System (NPS)
schemes.description: Voluntary defined contribution pension system administered by PFRDA. Offers market-linked returns with choice of fund managers and investment options. Provides tax benefits and retirement income.
schemes.eligibility: Indian citizens (resident or NRI) between 18-70 years. Two types: Tier I (mandatory for government employees, voluntary for others) with lock-in till 60; Tier II (voluntary savings without lock-in).
schemes.benefits: Tax deduction up to Rs. 2 lakh (Rs. 1.5 lakh under 80CCD(1) + Rs. 50,000 under 80CCD(1B)). Choice of fund managers and asset allocation. Minimum 40% corpus for annuity. Partial withdrawals allowed for specified purposes.
schemes.target_group: All citizens, government employees, private sector employees

schemes.name: PM Vishwakarma Yojana
schemes.description: Central sector scheme to provide end-to-end support to artisans and craftspeople working with their hands and tools across 18 traditional trades through recognition, skill upgradation, toolkit incentive, credit support, and marketing assistance.
schemes.eligibility: Artisans/craftspeople aged 18+ working in 18 identified trades (carpenter, blacksmith, goldsmith, potter, sculptor, cobbler, tailor, basket/mat maker, doll maker, barber, garland maker, washerman, etc.). Should work with hands and tools, self-employed, not in formal employment.
schemes.benefits: PM Vishwakarma certificate and ID card. Skill training with stipend of Rs. 500/day. Toolkit incentive of Rs. 15,000. Collateral-free credit: Rs. 1 lakh (1st tranche) + Rs. 2 lakh (2nd tranche) at 5% interest. Marketing and digital support.

schemes.name: PM Garib Kalyan Anna Yojana (PMGKAY)
schemes.description: World's largest food security program providing free foodgrains to around 81.35 crore beneficiaries covered under the National Food Security Act. Ensures food security for poor and vulnerable families.
schemes.eligibility: All beneficiaries covered under NFSA - Antyodaya Anna Yojana (AAY) households and Priority Household (PHH) beneficiaries as per state identification. Includes poorest of poor, widows, terminally ill, disabled, and destitute.
schemes.benefits: 5 kg free foodgrains per person per month (rice/wheat/coarse grains). AAY households: 35 kg per household per month. Can be availed through any Fair Price Shop using Aadhaar authentication.
documents: Aadhaar linked to ration card for biometric authentication
schemes.target_group: BPL families, AAY households, poor and vulnerable

schemes.name: Jal Jeevan Mission
schemes.description: Flagship program to provide safe and adequate drinking water through individual household tap connections to all rural households in India. Aims to ensure Functional Household Tap Connection (FHTC) to every rural home.
schemes.eligibility: All rural households without functional tap water connection. Priority to SC/ST dominant habitations, quality-affected areas, aspirational districts, drought-prone areas, and Sansad Adarsh Gram Yojana villages.
schemes.benefits: Functional Household Tap Connection providing 55 liters per capita per day (LPCD) of potable water. Grey water management infrastructure. Water quality monitoring and surveillance systems.

schemes.name: PM SVANidhi (Street Vendor)
schemes.description: Special micro-credit facility for street vendors to resume their livelihoods affected by COVID-19 lockdowns. Provides affordable working capital loans with incentives for digital transactions.
schemes.eligibility: Street vendors engaged in vending in urban areas as on or before March 24, 2020. Vendors on surrounding development/peri-urban areas. Must have Certificate of Vending/ID card from ULB or Letter of Recommendation.
schemes.benefits: Working capital loan: 1st loan Rs. 10,000, 2nd loan Rs. 20,000, 3rd loan Rs. 50,000. Interest subsidy of 7%. Cashback up to Rs. 1,200 per year for digital transactions. No collateral required.

schemes.name: MGNREGA (Mahatma Gandhi NREGA)
schemes.description: Largest work guarantee program in the world providing legal guarantee of 100 days of wage employment per year to every rural household willing to do unskilled manual work. Acts as social safety net and builds rural infrastructure.
schemes.eligibility: Adult members of rural households willing to do unskilled manual work. Must apply for Job Card. Work to be provided within 15 days of application, else unemployment allowance payable.
schemes.benefits: Guaranteed 100 days of wage employment per household per year. Wages as per state-wise notified rates (Rs. 213 to Rs. 374 per day). Work within 5 km of residence. Worksite facilities including creche, drinking water, first aid.

schemes.name: PM Matru Vandana Yojana (PMMVY)
schemes.description: Maternity benefit program providing cash incentive to pregnant and lactating mothers for first live birth. Aims to provide partial wage compensation for wage loss during childbirth and childcare.
schemes.eligibility: Pregnant women and lactating mothers for first live birth. Should be 19 years or above. Last date of registration: 730 days from LMP. Not applicable for government/PSU employees receiving paid maternity leave.
schemes.benefits: Cash incentive of Rs. 5,000 in three installments: Rs. 1,000 after early registration of pregnancy; Rs. 2,000 after six months of pregnancy (one ANC); Rs. 2,000 after birth registration and first cycle vaccination. Additional Rs. 6,000 for institutional delivery under JSY.
documents: MCP Card (Mother and Child Protection Card)

schemes.name: Poshan Abhiyaan (National Nutrition Mission)
schemes.description: Flagship program to improve nutritional outcomes for children, pregnant women, and lactating mothers. Uses technology, convergence, and community-based approach to address malnutrition.
schemes.eligibility: Children aged 0-6 years, pregnant women, and lactating mothers. Adolescent girls (11-14 years) in aspirational districts. Services through Anganwadi centers and primary health centers.
schemes.benefits: Supplementary nutrition through Anganwadi. Growth monitoring and promotion. Immunization and health check-ups. Referral services. Nutrition and health education. Access to fortified foods.
schemes.target_group: Children under 6, pregnant women, lactating mothers, adolescent girls

schemes.name: National Social Assistance Programme (NSAP)
schemes.description: Centrally sponsored scheme providing social pension and assistance to destitute elderly, widows, and persons with disabilities. Includes IGNOAPS, IGNWPS, IGNDPS, NFBS, and Annapurna schemes.
schemes.eligibility: BPL persons: Elderly (60+ years) for old age pension; Widows (40-79 years) for widow pension; Disabled (18-79 years) with 80%+ disability for disability pension. Family of deceased (18-59 years BPL breadwinner) for death assistance.
schemes.benefits: IGNOAPS: Rs. 200/month (60-79 years), Rs. 500/month (80+ years). IGNWPS: Rs. 300/month (widows 40-79). IGNDPS: Rs. 300/month (disabled 18-79). NFBS: Rs. 20,000 one-time (death of breadwinner). Annapurna: 10 kg free foodgrains/month for uncovered elderly.
schemes.target_group: Destitute elderly, widows, disabled persons, BPL families

schemes.name: Deendayal Antyodaya Yojana - NRLM
schemes.description: National Rural Livelihoods Mission aims to reduce poverty by enabling poor households to access gainful self-employment and skilled wage employment through building strong grassroots institutions of the poor (SHGs).
schemes.eligibility: Rural poor households, especially women. Target groups include those engaged in vulnerable occupations, landless, SC/ST, disabled, minority, etc. Organized into Self Help Groups (SHGs).
schemes.benefits: Revolving Fund of Rs. 10,000-15,000 per SHG. Community Investment Fund of Rs. 50,000-1 lakh per SHG. Bank linkage facilitation. Skill training and placement support. Interest subvention on loans. Vulnerability Reduction Fund.

schemes.name: Deendayal Antyodaya Yojana - NULM
schemes.description: National Urban Livelihoods Mission aims to reduce poverty and vulnerability of urban poor by enabling them to access gainful self-employment and skilled wage employment and building strong grassroots institutions.
schemes.eligibility: Urban poor and homeless in statutory towns. Self Help Groups, Area Level Federations, and City Level Federations of urban poor women. Unemployed youth for skill training. Street vendors for livelihood support.
schemes.benefits: Interest subsidy of 5-7% on bank loans for SHG/individual enterprise. Skill training with placement linkage. Shelter for urban homeless. Social mobilization and institutional development support. Revolving fund of Rs. 10,000 per SHG.

schemes.name: PM Fasal Bima Yojana (PMFBY)
schemes.description: Comprehensive crop insurance scheme providing financial support to farmers suffering crop loss/damage due to unforeseen events like natural calamities, pests, and diseases. Operates on area approach basis.
schemes.eligibility: All farmers including sharecroppers and tenant farmers growing notified crops in notified areas. Compulsory for loanee farmers, voluntary for non-loanee farmers. Both individual and group insurance available.
schemes.benefits: Low premium: Kharif 2%, Rabi 1.5%, Horticulture/Commercial 5% of sum insured. Balance premium paid by government. Coverage from pre-sowing to post-harvest. Add-on coverage for localized calamities, mid-season adversity, and prevented sowing.

schemes.name: Kisan Credit Card (KCC)
schemes.description: Credit scheme for farmers to provide timely access to credit for agricultural needs including crop production, post-harvest expenses, consumption, working capital for maintenance of farm assets, and allied activities.
schemes.eligibility: All farmers (individuals/joint) who are owner cultivators, tenant farmers, oral lessees, or share croppers. Self Help Groups or Joint Liability Groups of farmers. Also extended to fisheries and animal husbandry.
schemes.benefits: Credit limit based on landholding, cropping pattern, and scale of finance. 2% interest subvention on timely repayment (effective rate 4% p.a. for crop loans up to Rs. 3 lakh). Personal accident insurance of Rs. 50,000. Flexible repayment options.
documents: PAN Card (for loans above Rs. 1 lakh)
For allied activities: Registration/License as applicable
schemes.target_group: Farmers, fishermen, dairy farmers, poultry farmers

schemes.name: PM Kisan Maan Dhan Yojana
schemes.description: Pension scheme for small and marginal farmers. Provides minimum pension of Rs. 3,000 per month on attaining 60 years of age. Voluntary and contributory scheme with matching contribution by government.
schemes.eligibility: Small and marginal farmers aged 18-40 years with cultivable land up to 2 hectares as per land records. Should not be covered under PM Shram Yogi Maandhan, PM Laghu Vyapari Maandhan, NPS, or ESIC.
schemes.benefits: Guaranteed minimum pension of Rs. 3,000 per month after 60 years. Equal pension to spouse after subscriber's death. If subscriber dies before 60: spouse can continue with contribution or exit with subscriber's contribution plus interest. Government contributes equal amount.
documents: Land Records showing holding up to 2 hectares

schemes.name: PM Shram Yogi Maandhan Yojana
schemes.description: Pension scheme for unorganized workers. Provides guaranteed minimum pension of Rs. 3,000 per month after 60 years for workers in unorganized sector like street vendors, domestic workers, construction workers, etc.
schemes.eligibility: Unorganized workers aged 18-40 years with monthly income up to Rs. 15,000. Should not be covered under ESIC, EPFO, NPS (government), or PM Kisan Maandhan. Entry age determines contribution (Rs. 55 to Rs. 200 per month).
schemes.benefits: Guaranteed minimum pension of Rs. 3,000 per month from age 60. Same pension to spouse after death. Option to exit with accumulated contribution plus interest. Government contributes matching amount.
schemes.target_group: Unorganized workers, street vendors, domestic workers, construction workers

schemes.name: E-Shram Card (Unorganized Workers)
schemes.description: National database for unorganized workers to provide them a portable social security system. Registration provides a unique 12-digit Universal Account Number (UAN) and e-Shram card enabling access to various welfare schemes.
schemes.eligibility: Unorganized workers aged 16-59 years not a member of ESIC or EPFO. Includes construction workers, gig workers, platform workers, street vendors, domestic workers, agricultural workers, ASHA/Anganwadi workers, etc.
schemes.benefits: Unique e-Shram Card with 12-digit UAN. Accidental insurance of Rs. 2 lakh (death) and Rs. 1 lakh (partial disability) under PMSBY. Access to various government schemes. Portability across states.

schemes.name: Agnipath Scheme (Agniveer)
schemes.description: Recruitment scheme for Indian Armed Forces (Army, Navy, Air Force) that enables patriotic and motivated youth to serve in the Armed Forces for a period of four years. Provides financial package and various benefits.
schemes.eligibility: Indian citizens aged 17.5 to 21 years (upper age relaxed to 23 for some years). Educational qualification: 10th pass to graduate depending on category. Must meet physical and medical standards.
schemes.benefits: Monthly package: Rs. 30,000 (1st year) to Rs. 40,000 (4th year). 30% of package contributed to Seva Nidhi corpus with matching government contribution. Tax-free lump sum of Rs. 11.71 lakh on completion. Skill certificate. Preference in government jobs for 25% retained.
schemes.target_group: Youth seeking career in Armed Forces

schemes.name: Ladli Behna Yojana
schemes.description: Madhya Pradesh government's flagship scheme to promote economic empowerment and financial independence of women. Provides direct monthly cash transfer to eligible women irrespective of caste or religion.
schemes.eligibility: Women aged 21-60 years who are residents of Madhya Pradesh. Family annual income should be less than Rs. 2.5 lakh. Should not be income tax payer. Should not have 4-wheeler vehicle (tractor exempt). Should not be government employee/pensioner.
schemes.benefits: Monthly financial assistance of Rs. 1,250 (increased from Rs. 1,000). Direct transfer to Aadhaar-linked bank account. Annual benefit of Rs. 15,000. Covers married, widowed, divorced, and abandoned women.

schemes.name: Ladki Bahin Yojana (Maharashtra)
schemes.description: Maharashtra government's scheme for economic empowerment of women, inspired by MP's Ladli Behna. Provides monthly cash assistance to eligible women for financial independence and improved living standards.
schemes.eligibility: Women aged 21-65 years who are domiciled in Maharashtra. Annual family income below Rs. 2.5 lakh. Should not be income tax payer. Should not own more than 5 acres of land.
schemes.benefits: Monthly financial assistance of Rs. 1,500. Direct bank transfer through DBT. Annual benefit of Rs. 18,000. Covers married, unmarried, widowed, divorced, and abandoned women.

schemes.name: Mahtari Vandana Yojana (Chhattisgarh)
schemes.description: Chhattisgarh government's scheme providing monthly financial assistance to married women for their economic empowerment and improved social status in the family.
schemes.eligibility: Married women aged 21-60 years who are permanent residents of Chhattisgarh. Annual family income below Rs. 2.5 lakh. Should not be government employee or income tax payer.
schemes.benefits: Monthly financial assistance of Rs. 1,000. Annual benefit of Rs. 12,000 through direct bank transfer. Covers married, widowed, divorced, and abandoned women.

schemes.name: Kanya Sumangala Yojana (UP)
schemes.description: Uttar Pradesh government's scheme to provide financial assistance to families for the welfare of girl children from birth to graduation. Aims to eliminate negative attitudes towards the girl child.
schemes.eligibility: Girl children born on or after April 1, 2019 in families domiciled in UP. Family annual income should not exceed Rs. 3 lakh. Maximum two girls per family (relaxation for twin girls in second pregnancy).
schemes.benefits: Total Rs. 25,000 in six stages: Rs. 5,000 at birth; Rs. 2,000 on completing 1 year vaccination; Rs. 3,000 on admission to Class 1; Rs. 3,000 on admission to Class 6; Rs. 5,000 on admission to Class 9; Rs. 7,000 on admission to graduation/2-year diploma.

schemes.name: Mukhyamantri Kanya Vivah Yojana (Bihar)
schemes.description: Bihar government's scheme to provide financial assistance for marriage of girls from economically weaker families. Aims to promote girl education, prevent child marriage, and reduce dowry-related issues.
schemes.eligibility: Bride should be 18 years or above, groom 21 years or above at time of marriage. Family should be BPL or have annual income below Rs. 60,000. Must be domiciled in Bihar. Marriage should be registered.
schemes.benefits: Financial assistance of Rs. 5,000 to eligible bride. Amount transferred directly to bride's bank account after marriage registration. One-time grant per girl.
schemes.target_group: Girls from poor families in Bihar

schemes.name: RTE Act Free Education
schemes.description: Right of Children to Free and Compulsory Education Act mandates free and compulsory education for all children aged 6-14 years. Requires private schools to reserve 25% seats for disadvantaged groups.
schemes.eligibility: Children aged 6-14 years for free education in government schools. For 25% reservation in private schools: Children from weaker sections (EWS with income below Rs. 1-3.5 lakh depending on state) and disadvantaged groups (SC/ST/OBC/Disabled).
schemes.benefits: Free elementary education from Class 1 to 8 in government schools. 25% seats in private unaided schools at no cost. Free textbooks, uniforms (in some states). No detention till Class 8. Mid-day meals in schools.
schemes.application_url: Varies by state - apply through state education portals

schemes.name: Post Matric Scholarship for SC Students
schemes.description: Centrally sponsored scheme providing financial assistance to SC students studying at post-matriculation level to enable them to complete their education. Includes maintenance allowance and fee reimbursement.
schemes.eligibility: Students belonging to Scheduled Castes studying at post-matriculation (Class 11) to PhD level. Parents'/guardians' income should not exceed Rs. 2.5 lakh per annum. Should be domiciled in the state of application.
schemes.benefits: Full reimbursement of compulsory fees. Maintenance allowance: Rs. 230-1,200 per month (day scholars), Rs. 550-3,000 per month (hostellers) depending on course level. Books and study material allowance.

schemes.name: Post Matric Scholarship for ST Students
schemes.description: Centrally sponsored scheme providing financial assistance to ST students studying at post-matriculation level. Covers tuition fees, maintenance allowance, and other expenses to reduce dropout rates.
schemes.eligibility: Students belonging to Scheduled Tribes studying at post-matriculation to PhD level. Parents'/guardians' annual income should not exceed Rs. 2.5 lakh. Should be domiciled in the state of application.
schemes.benefits: Full reimbursement of compulsory non-refundable fees. Maintenance allowance varying from Rs. 230 to Rs. 1,200 per month for day scholars and Rs. 550 to Rs. 3,000 for hostellers. Additional allowance for books and ad-hoc grants.

schemes.name: Post Matric Scholarship for OBC Students
schemes.description: Centrally sponsored scheme providing financial assistance to OBC students studying at post-matriculation level to enable them to complete education without financial constraints.
schemes.eligibility: Students belonging to Other Backward Classes studying at post-matriculation to PhD level. Parents'/guardians' annual income should not exceed Rs. 1.5 lakh. Should not be availing any other scholarship.
schemes.benefits: Maintenance allowance: Rs. 260-750 per month (day scholars), Rs. 750-1,000 per month (hostellers). Additional reader allowance for blind students. Exemption from tuition fee and compulsory fees.

schemes.name: National Scholarship for Persons with Disabilities
schemes.description: Scholarship scheme under Accessible India Campaign to support students with disabilities in pursuing education from Class 9 onwards. Provides financial assistance for tuition, books, and special equipment.
schemes.eligibility: Students with 40%+ disability (locomotor, visual, hearing, intellectual, mental illness, multiple disabilities) studying in Class 9 onwards. Family income should not exceed Rs. 2.5 lakh per annum.
schemes.benefits: Class 9-12: Rs. 500 per month (day scholar), Rs. 1,000 per month (hosteller). Graduate/Professional courses: Rs. 1,000-3,000 per month. Allowances for books, escort, reader (for blind), and special equipment.

schemes.name: UDID Card (Unique Disability ID)
schemes.description: National database of Persons with Disabilities (PwDs) creating a unique identification for each PwD. UDID card serves as a single document for availing various benefits and schemes across the country.
schemes.eligibility: Any person with one or more of the 21 specified disabilities under RPwD Act 2016 with at least 40% disability as certified by competent authority. Includes locomotor, visual, hearing, intellectual disabilities, autism, mental illness, etc.
schemes.benefits: Single document for PwD identification across India. Ease of access to all government schemes and benefits. Tracking of benefits delivery. Reduced paperwork for availing services. Accepted by all government departments.

schemes.name: Indira Gandhi National Widow Pension Scheme
schemes.description: Central assistance to states for providing pension to widows belonging to BPL households. Part of National Social Assistance Programme providing basic income security to destitute widows.
schemes.eligibility: Widows aged 40-79 years belonging to BPL households. Should be identified under state BPL criteria. Not receiving any other pension. Verification through local authorities.
schemes.benefits: Central contribution of Rs. 300 per month per beneficiary. States provide additional amount (varies Rs. 200-2,000 depending on state). Total pension typically Rs. 500-2,300 per month.
documents: Certificate from local authorities confirming widow status

schemes.name: Indira Gandhi National Disability Pension Scheme
schemes.description: Central assistance for providing pension to persons with severe and multiple disabilities belonging to BPL households. Part of NSAP ensuring basic financial security for destitute disabled.
schemes.eligibility: Persons with 80%+ disability aged 18-79 years belonging to BPL households. Disability should be certified by competent medical authority. Multiple disabilities also covered.
schemes.benefits: Central contribution of Rs. 300 per month per beneficiary. States provide additional amount (varies Rs. 200-2,000). Total pension typically Rs. 500-2,500 per month depending on state.
schemes.target_group: BPL persons with severe disability aged 18-79 years

schemes.name: Swadhar Greh Scheme
schemes.description: Scheme for rehabilitation of women in difficult circumstances including widows, destitute women, survivors of natural disasters, trafficking victims, released women prisoners, and women with mental illness.
schemes.eligibility: Women above 18 years in difficult circumstances: deserted/abandoned women, homeless, victims of domestic violence, trafficked women, women with mental illness, women prisoners, women affected by HIV/AIDS, etc.
schemes.benefits: Shelter, food, clothing, and care. Medical facilities including counseling. Legal aid and guidance. Vocational and skill training. Rehabilitation and reintegration support. Helpline support.
documents: No specific documents mandatory - can be admitted on recommendation

schemes.name: Ujjawala Scheme (Anti-Trafficking)
schemes.description: Comprehensive scheme for prevention of trafficking and rescue, rehabilitation, re-integration, and repatriation of victims of trafficking and commercial sexual exploitation.
schemes.eligibility: Women and children who are victims of trafficking or commercial sexual exploitation. Potential victims in source/transit/destination areas. Children of victims who are vulnerable.
schemes.benefits: Rescue and safe withdrawal from place of exploitation. Rehabilitation in protective homes with shelter, food, clothing, medical care, counseling. Repatriation and re-integration with family/society. Skill training for income generation.
documents: Documents not mandatory for rescue and rehabilitation

schemes.name: YSR Cheyutha (Andhra Pradesh)
schemes.description: Andhra Pradesh government's scheme providing annual financial assistance to women aged 45-60 years from SC, ST, BC, and Minority communities to support their livelihood and improve economic status.
schemes.eligibility: Women aged 45-60 years belonging to SC/ST/BC/Minority communities who are residents of Andhra Pradesh. Family annual income should be below Rs. 2.5 lakh. Should not be government employee.
schemes.benefits: Annual financial assistance of Rs. 18,500 (increased from Rs. 18,750). Amount transferred directly to beneficiary's bank account. Covers women for 15 years from age 45 to 60.
schemes.target_group: Women aged 45-60 from SC/ST/BC/Minority in Andhra Pradesh

schemes.name: YSR Pension Kanuka (Andhra Pradesh)
schemes.description: Comprehensive social security pension scheme of Andhra Pradesh providing monthly pension to elderly, widows, weavers, toddy tappers, fishermen, single women, persons with disabilities, and other vulnerable groups.
schemes.eligibility: Various categories: Old age (60+ years), Widows (18+ years), Disabled (18+ years with 40%+ disability), Weavers, Toddy Tappers, Fishermen, Single women (35+ years), Transgender persons, etc. Family income criteria apply.
schemes.benefits: Monthly pension of Rs. 2,500-2,750 depending on category. Paid through door-step delivery on 1st of every month. Covers over 60 lakh beneficiaries across the state.
schemes.target_group: Elderly, widows, disabled, weavers, fishermen, single women in AP

schemes.name: Aasara Pension (Telangana)
schemes.description: Telangana government's social security pension scheme providing monthly financial assistance to elderly, widows, persons with disabilities, beedi workers, toddy tappers, single women, and AIDS patients.
schemes.eligibility: Various categories: Old age (65+ years), Widows (18+ years), Disabled (40%+ disability), Beedi workers, Toddy tappers, Single women (30-65 years), Filaria patients, AIDS patients. Income limit Rs. 1.5 lakh for rural, Rs. 2 lakh for urban.
schemes.benefits: Monthly pension: Rs. 2,016 for old age/widows/disabled/single women/AIDS patients. Paid through direct bank transfer. Special higher pension for certain categories.
schemes.target_group: Elderly, widows, disabled, vulnerable groups in Telangana

schemes.name: Kalyana Lakshmi (Telangana)
schemes.description: Telangana government's scheme providing one-time financial assistance for marriage of girls from economically weaker families belonging to SC, ST, BC, and Minority communities.
schemes.eligibility: Bride should be 18+ years and groom 21+ years. Bride should belong to SC/ST/BC/Minority community and be a resident of Telangana for 10+ years. Family annual income should not exceed Rs. 2 lakh.
schemes.benefits: One-time financial assistance of Rs. 1,00,116 for eligible marriages. Amount transferred directly to bride's bank account. Additional Rs. 10,000 if bride is graduate.
schemes.target_group: Girls from SC/ST/BC/Minority families in Telangana

schemes.name: Shaadi Mubarak (Telangana)
schemes.description: Marriage assistance scheme for Muslim girls in Telangana providing financial assistance at the time of marriage to reduce financial burden on poor families.
schemes.eligibility: Muslim girls aged 18+ years who are residents of Telangana. Family annual income should not exceed Rs. 2 lakh. Marriage should be first marriage of the girl.
schemes.benefits: One-time financial assistance of Rs. 1,00,116 for marriage. Amount transferred to bride's bank account. Can be combined with central government schemes.

schemes.name: Kalia Yojana (Odisha)
schemes.description: Krushak Assistance for Livelihood and Income Augmentation (KALIA) scheme of Odisha providing financial, livelihood, and insurance support to farmers, landless agricultural laborers, and vulnerable agricultural households.
schemes.eligibility: Small and marginal farmers with cultivable land up to 5 acres. Landless agricultural households. Vulnerable agricultural households (elderly, disabled, diseased). Should be resident of Odisha.
schemes.benefits: Cultivators: Rs. 4,000 per family in each Kharif and Rabi season (Rs. 8,000 annually). Landless laborers: Rs. 12,500 for livelihood activities. Vulnerable households: Rs. 10,000 per family per year. Life insurance of Rs. 2 lakh and personal accident cover of Rs. 2 lakh.

schemes.name: Rythu Bandhu (Telangana)
schemes.description: Investment support scheme of Telangana providing financial assistance to farmers for purchase of inputs like seeds, fertilizers, and other investments. Direct benefit transfer to farmer's account.
schemes.eligibility: All farmers (cultivators) with agricultural land ownership in Telangana as per land records. No ceiling on land holdings. Includes pattadars of assigned lands.
schemes.benefits: Rs. 10,000 per acre per year (Rs. 5,000 per season for Kharif and Rabi each). No cap on total amount based on land holding. Amount credited directly to bank account before cropping season.

schemes.name: Rythu Bima (Telangana)
schemes.description: Life insurance scheme for farmers in Telangana providing accidental death coverage and life insurance to protect farmer families from financial distress in case of death of the farmer.
schemes.eligibility: All farmers aged 18-59 years in Telangana who are enrolled in Rythu Bandhu scheme. Premium paid entirely by state government.
schemes.benefits: Life insurance cover of Rs. 5 lakhs in case of death (natural or accidental). Premium of Rs. 2,271 per year paid by government. Claims settled within 7 days of application.

schemes.name: Mukhyamantri Sukanya Yojana (Jharkhand)
schemes.description: Jharkhand government's scheme for girl children providing financial assistance from birth till completion of 18 years. Aims to improve female literacy rate and reduce child marriage.
schemes.eligibility: Girl children born in Jharkhand. Family should be below poverty line (BPL) or have annual income less than specified limit. Maximum two girls per family. Should be enrolled in school.
schemes.benefits: Total Rs. 40,000 from birth to 18 years in six installments: Rs. 5,000 at birth; Rs. 5,000 on completion of 1 year; Rs. 5,000 on admission to Class 1; Rs. 5,000 on admission to Class 6; Rs. 5,000 on admission to Class 9; Rs. 20,000 on completing 18 years and passing Class 12.

schemes.name: PM CARES for Children Scheme
schemes.description: Scheme to support children who lost both parents or legal guardian/adoptive parent/surviving parent to COVID-19. Provides comprehensive support for health, education, and financial needs till age 23.
schemes.eligibility: Children who lost both parents or legal guardian/adoptive parent/surviving parent to COVID-19 between March 11, 2020 and December 31, 2021 (extended dates). Child should be below 18 years on date of parent's death.
schemes.benefits: Corpus of Rs. 10 lakh per child at age 18 through PM CARES fund. Monthly stipend till age 18. Free education in government schools/colleges or fee reimbursement in private institutions. Free health insurance of Rs. 5 lakh under Ayushman Bharat.

schemes.name: Mukhyamantri Majhi Ladki Bahin Yojana (Maharashtra)
schemes.description: Maharashtra government scheme providing monthly financial assistance to women for their economic empowerment and to improve household living standards.
schemes.eligibility: Women aged 21-65 years who are residents of Maharashtra. Annual family income below Rs. 2.5 lakh. Should not be income tax payer. Should not be government employee or receiving pension above Rs. 1 lakh.
schemes.benefits: Monthly financial assistance of Rs. 1,500 (Rs. 18,000 per year). Direct bank transfer to Aadhaar-linked account. Benefits married, widowed, divorced, and abandoned women.

schemes.name: Nari Samman Yojana (Himachal Pradesh)
schemes.description: Himachal Pradesh government scheme providing monthly financial assistance to women for their economic empowerment and financial independence.
schemes.eligibility: Women aged 18 years and above who are permanent residents of Himachal Pradesh. Should not be income tax payer. Should not be government employee. Family annual income criteria as per state norms.
schemes.benefits: Monthly financial assistance of Rs. 1,500 per month. Direct transfer to Aadhaar-linked bank account. Annual benefit of Rs. 18,000.
documents: Bank Account Details linked to Aadhaar

schemes.name: National Family Benefit Scheme (NFBS)
schemes.description: One-time lump sum assistance to bereaved household on the death of primary breadwinner. Part of National Social Assistance Programme supporting families who lose earning members.
schemes.eligibility: Household living below poverty line. Primary breadwinner (male or female) aged 18-59 years who substantially contributed to household income dies (natural or accidental death). Claim within one year of death.
schemes.benefits: One-time lump sum assistance of Rs. 20,000 to bereaved household. Assistance provided to bereaved spouse or eldest surviving member of household.
documents: Income contribution proof of deceased (if available)
schemes.target_group: BPL families who lost primary breadwinner

schemes.name: Annapurna Scheme
schemes.description: Scheme to provide free foodgrains to destitute senior citizens who are eligible but not receiving pension under National Old Age Pension Scheme. Ensures food security for the poorest elderly.
schemes.eligibility: Destitute senior citizens aged 65 years and above. Should be eligible for but not receiving pension under IGNOAPS/state pension. Should be destitute with no regular means of subsistence from own sources or family.
schemes.benefits: 10 kg of free foodgrains (rice or wheat) per month per beneficiary. Distributed through Public Distribution System.
documents: Declaration of not receiving old age pension
Destitute status verification from local authorities
schemes.target_group: Destitute elderly not covered under pension schemes
